mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a tightening monetary policy aimed at cooling an overheating economy. This dual pressure increases borrowing costs and reduces purchasing power, negatively impacting corporate earnings and equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate healthy investment and consumer confidence, which translates into broader economic activity and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is not significantly positive during periods where GDPC1 year-over-year growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating a weakening economy that will negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods following a 3-month average decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate profitability, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is high and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, it suggests the central bank is actively trying to curb inflation in a strong economy. This tight monetary stance can eventually lead to a slowdown, negatively impacting equity markets over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% and UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth accelerates by > 1pp in 2 quarters
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where GDPC1 year-over-year growth accelerates by more than 1 percentage point over two quarters.
